paper_title: Phytoplankton Distribution and Analysis in the Baltic Sea

methods.description: Process for collecting, unifying, and harmonizing phytoplankton data from various national monitoring agencies around the Baltic Sea.

steps.description: The spatial and temporal distribution of phytoplankton was obtained from monitoring datasets provided by national monitoring agencies around the Baltic Sea.
steps.searchable_summary: collect phytoplankton distribution data from national monitoring agencies

steps.description: The original data tables from national agencies or international organizations (HELCOM) were structurally unified and the taxonomy was carefully harmonized.
steps.searchable_summary: unify data structure and harmonize taxonomy

steps.description: Phytoplankton data were counted from Lugol fixed, surface mixed layer integrated samples (0-5 or 0-10 m) with an inverted microscope after settling for 24 h.
steps.searchable_summary: count phytoplankton from Lugol fixed samples using inverted microscope

steps.description: Species-specific cell volumes were used to calculate the wet weight biomass.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate wet weight biomass using species-specific cell volumes

methods.description: Statistical procedures used to analyze the collected phytoplankton data using the R computing environment.

steps.description: All statistics were done in the R computing environment.
